pattern: Group buildings by row and by column, track the extreme coordinates of each line, and a building is covered exactly when it lies strictly between the min and max in both its row and its column.
hints: A building is covered if some building shares its row to the left and another to the right, and likewise some building shares its column above and below.
For each row, the only thing that matters is the smallest and largest column present; for each column, the smallest and largest row present.
Precompute per-row min/max column and per-column min/max row, then a building [x, y] is covered when min_col[x] < y < max_col[x] and min_row[y] < x < max_row[y].

edge_inputs.description: the canonical plus shape covers its center
a 2x2 block has nothing strictly between, so none are covered
spread plus shape on a larger board
has neighbors on three sides only, so not covered
two buildings covered along the same central row
all in one row so no vertical neighbours exist

stress.description: many random distinct buildings on a moderate grid so dense lines create real coverage
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(50, size))
    count = min(n * n, max(1, size))
    cells = set()
    while len(cells) < count:
        x = rng.randint(1, n)
        y = rng.randint(1, n)
        cells.add((x, y))
    buildings = [[x, y] for (x, y) in cells]
    rng.shuffle(buildings)
    return [n, buildings]
